notes_md:
**tim** = heart (the body organ that pumps blood). It also means your emotional “heart” — feelings, love, courage.

It’s a noun. You’ll often hear **trái tim** (more complete, emotional tone) and sometimes **quả tim** (more physical/medical).  

Common pairings: **nhịp tim** (heartbeat), **đau tim** (heart pain / heart attack in casual talk), **tận đáy tim** (from the bottom of one’s heart).  

Metaphorically, **tim** can mean the emotional center of something, like the “heart” of a problem or a city.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I feel my heart beating very fast when I meet you.
The doctor said the health of the heart is very important for life.
When sad, she often hugs her chest where the heart is to feel it.

definitions.en: The organ in your chest that sends the blood around your body.
definitions.es: El órgano en tu pecho que envía la sangre por todo tu cuerpo.
definitions.vi: Cơ quan trong ngực của bạn gửi máu đi khắp cơ thể.

example.en: I feel my heart beating fast.
example.vi: Tôi cảm thấy tim mình đập nhanh.

notes:
tim = heart (the body organ that pumps blood). It also means your emotional “heart” — feelings, love, courage.

It’s a noun. You’ll often hear trái tim (more complete, emotional tone) and sometimes quả tim (more physical/medical).  

Common pairings: nhịp tim (heartbeat), đau tim (heart pain / heart attack in casual talk), tận đáy tim (from the bottom of one’s heart).  

Metaphorically, tim can mean the emotional center of something, like the “heart” of a problem or a city.
exampleSentences: Em cảm thấy tim mình đập rất nhanh khi gặp anh.
Bác sĩ nói sức khỏe của tim rất quan trọng cho cuộc sống.
Khi buồn, chị ấy thường ôm ngực nơi trái tim để cảm nhận.
